items.title: "Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.4 "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound" Arrives on July 15"
items.summary: Version 4.4 gets all poetic with its "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound" title, Asat Pramad provides an immense challenge.
items.content:
SINGAPORE, July 3, 2026 — Global interactive entertainment brand HoYoverse announced today that the brand-new Version 4.4 of Honkai: Star Rail, In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound, will launch on July 15. This version will continue the story as Planarcadia faces a crisis that threatens its survival. The Trailblazer will confront the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad and engage in an epic battle. The second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration will also commence in this version. As another Holy Grail War begins, embark on a special chapter across the stars with Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh. Tap here to watch the latest trailer: https://youtu.be/xT88aPknvag Tap here to obtain the media kit: https://hoyoverse.box.com/s/lm1nq9c3od109wm31fpqnb37kwfa4kdf At the end of the previous chapter, the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad unveiled his disguise to reveal his true self and shattered the peace that had been sustained for fifteen years in Planarcadia. After the conflict broke out, Himeko vanished during her confrontation with Asat Pramad. Before she disappeared, she left behind a hidden clue related to the Express. In this version's story, the Trailblazer tracks down and deciphers the clue to locate Himeko. Asat Pramad's obsession with Himeko's identity and the truth from fifteen years ago may just be the key to unraveling his motive. Ultimately, the Trailblazer will face Asat Pramad in battle. Will Planarcadia survive this game of destruction threatening its existence? In this decisive battle, Himeko will join the fight in her new form: Himeko • Nova. The banner for the 5-star character Himeko • Nova will last throughout the entire Version 4.4. Himeko • Nova is a Fire-Type character following the Path of Erudition. When the battle begins, she immediately deploys a Territory and summons the mech "Starblazer" to assist in battle. When using her Ultimate, she leads her teammates to pilot "Starblazer" and attack enemies. When the Territory is deployed, all teammates additionally gain the Assist Skill, and they can summon "Starblazer" at any time to attack all enemies. If a teammate is part of the Express Crew, they can also gain special effects. Additionally, when using her Technique during overworld exploration, Himeko • Nova can also pilot her mech, aim at and attack enemies within range from high above in the sky. Planarcadia will also encounter an unexpected turn of events. After the end of the Fifth Holy Grail War, Rin Tohsaka embarks on a journey to another world and stumbles into Planarcadia as her magical energy runs out. In her desperation, she borrows the Holy Grail displayed as part of the Star Rail FEST collection showcase. She then attempts to summon a Servant and use the Holy Grail's power to leave this world. The overflowing Wishpower in this land restores the Holy Grail, and the King of Heroes Gilgamesh's treasures are scattered across the arcadia. A massive commotion breaks out as a result. Upon the request of the Department of Aberration Defense, the Trailblazer works with Rin Tohsaka to locate the treasures and uncover the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. The special chapter and second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration officially begin on July 24. Please stay tuned. As the Holy Grail War commences once more, Rin Tohsaka joins the team as a playable character. She is a 5-star Quantum-Type character following the Path of Erudition, skilled in using gems in battle. During combat, she gains a set amount of "Gem Energy" and deals multiple additional instances of DMG to a random target by consuming the "Gem Energy" she has. The more "Gem Energy" she consumes, the more instances of additional DMG she deals. When teammates consume or recover Skill Points, CRIT DMG also increases and Rin Tohsaka accumulates "Gem Energy." Gilgamesh will also become a playable character. He is a 5-star Lightning-Type character following the Path of Destruction. After entering battle, each ally unit taking action can accumulate "Interest" for Gilgamesh, regenerate a set amount of Energy, and increase the DMG of his Ultimate. When accumulated "Interest" reaches its threshold, Gilgamesh unlocks his Skill and unleashes Gate of Babylon to fire weapons from his treasury, dealing multiple instances of DMG to enemies. When using his Ultimate, Gilgamesh summons Enuma Elish to deal massive DMG to all enemies. From the start of the collaboration on July 24 until before Version 4.6 ends, Trailblazers can log into the game and claim Gilgamesh or Archer as one of the free limited 5-star collaboration characters, along with a portion of the corresponding upgrade materials for the character. The characters and Light Cones for this collaboration will be available long term, and the guarantee counts are shared between the Collaboration Character Warps of both phases. The same applies for the Collaboration Light Cone Warps. Spend a total of 200 Star Rail Special Passes in Version 4.4 to select and claim one of the limited 5-star collaboration Light Cone "I Am As You Behold" or "The Hell Where Ideals Burn" after the collaboration begins. Limited 5-star characters Sparxie, Dan Heng • Permansor Terrae, and Evernight will return in the first half of this version for a limited time. Cerydra, Anaxa, and Aventurine will return to Warp banners in the second half of this version. During this version, Sparxie's new outfit "Candy-Coated Waves" will also be available. A new pet "Pentaflavor Emoti," Trailblaze Fashion "Fan-Made Lygus," and other new content will also be added in this version, bringing even more surprises to the journey. Lastly, Version 4.4 will also offer a variety of new events and game modes. First up, players can log in to claim a 10-pull reward once the version goes live. After the version update, the collaboration event "Fate/Star Rail Night" will also unfold as the story progresses. The Trailblazer tracks down the king's treasures with Rin Tohsaka and gradually unravels the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. In the version event "Antigraft Brickbuster," the Trailblazer takes on a request from Aventurine and launches an investigation into IPC corruption by stacking data bricks. Additionally, the permanent gameplay feature "Currency Wars: Zero-Sum Game" will be expanded and updated. Some system features will also be optimized. Please stay tuned. Honkai: Star Rail is now available on PC, iOS, Android, Epic Games Store, and PS5™, and has exceeded 150 million total downloads across all platforms. In 2023, the game won Apple's App Store iPhone Game of the Year, Google Play's Best Game, and BEST MOBILE GAME by The Game Awards. In 2024, it received the Golden Joystick Awards Still Playing Award (Mobile) and PlayStation® Partner Awards 2024 PARTNER AWARD among other honors. The game has been rated Teen by the ESRB and is also classified as PEGI 12. If you wish to learn more details, please visit https://hsr.hoyoverse.com or follow @HonkaiStarRail on Twitter (X), Instagram, and Facebook. About Honkai: Star Rail Honkai: Star Rail is a space fantasy RPG title with a journey through immense worlds of the unknown. The player takes on the role of "Trailblazer," a courageous individual implanted with a powerful artifact, Stellaron, and bearing the determination to unravel the mysteries surrounding the fabled "Cancer of All Worlds" and Aeons. The game features fantasy elements with myths and legends integrated into the space sci-fi story. Combined with the intuitive turn-based combat system, diverse and distinctive companions, and immersive storylines that together compose an interstellar melody filled with captivating worlds, each brimming with unique cultures, awe-inspiring landscapes, and mythical wonders to explore. About HoYoverse HoYoverse is committed to providing immersive virtual world experiences to players around the world. We have brought fans popular games including Genshin Impact, Honkai: Star Rail, Honkai Impact 3rd, Tears of Themis, and Zenless Zone Zero, as well as a wide range of entertainment content. Community is at the heart of everything we do. We are devoted to engaging fans and fostering an enthusiastic and inclusive global community that provides access and encouragement for people to share their passion for ACG (Animation, Comics, and Games) through their own creativity and skills. Pushing the boundaries of imagination, we consistently explore cutting-edge game development technologies, and have accumulated leading technical capabilities in cel shading, cloud gaming, and other fields. In the future, we will continue to expand our content production, technology research, and publishing duties through operations in offices in Singapore, Montreal, Los Angeles, Tokyo, Seoul, and other areas. Media Contact: Muyang Lin: muyang.lin@hoyoverse.com (General Information) Sam Lai: zhengting.lai@hoyoverse.com (SEA) Steve Ruygrok: steve.ruygrok@hoyoverse.com (North America) Yifei Yang: yifei.yang02@hoyoverse.com (Japan) Eric Portnoy: eric.portnoy@hoyoverse.com (LATAM) Natacha Concordel: natacha.concordel@hoyoverse.com (Europe)

Don't have a login? If you work within the games trade (press, influencer, developer, publisher or PR agency) you can zarejestruj się tutaj .

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Reveals Information On New Fate/Stay Night Content
items.summary: Honkai: Star Rail has a new update on the horizon titled "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound." It will include new content for the Fate/Stay Night collaboration.
items.content:
Last month, Hoyoverse teased us with new banners that will come out once they update the Fate/Stay Night content. A new Rin and Gilgamesh banner. Today, we finally got information about what the content will be and when the new banners will be released.

Global interactive entertainment brand HoYoverse announced today that the brand-new Version 4.4 of Honkai: Star Rail, In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound, will launch on July 15. This version will continue the story as Planarcadia faces a crisis that threatens its survival. The Trailblazer will confront the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad and engage in an epic battle. The second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration will also commence in this version. As another Holy Grail War begins, embark on a special chapter across the stars with Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh.

At the end of the previous chapter, the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad unveiled his disguise to reveal his true self and shattered the peace that had been sustained for fifteen years in Planarcadia. After the conflict broke out, Himeko vanished during her confrontation with Asat Pramad. Before she disappeared, she left behind a hidden clue related to the Express. In this version’s story, the Trailblazer tracks down and deciphers the clue to locate Himeko. Asat Pramad’s obsession with Himeko’s identity and the truth from fifteen years ago may just be the key to unraveling his motive. Ultimately, the Trailblazer will face Asat Pramad in battle. Will Planarcadia survive this game of destruction threatening its existence?

In this decisive battle, Himeko will join the fight in her new form: Himeko Nova. The banner for the 5 star character Himeko Nova will last throughout the entire Version 4.4. Himeko Nova is a Fire-Type character following the Path of Erudition. When the battle begins, she immediately deploys a Territory and summons the mech “Starblazer” to assist in battle. When using her Ultimate, she leads her teammates to pilot “Starblazer” and attack enemies. When the Territory is deployed, all teammates additionally gain the Assist Skill, and they can summon “Starblazer” at any time to attack all enemies. If a teammate is part of the Express Crew, they can also gain special effects. Additionally, when using her Technique during overworld exploration, Himeko Nova can also pilot her mech, aim at and attack enemies within range from high above in the sky.

Planarcadia will also encounter an unexpected turn of events. After the end of the Fifth Holy Grail War, Rin Tohsaka embarks on a journey to another world and stumbles into Planarcadia as her magical energy runs out. In her desperation, she borrows the Holy Grail displayed as part of the Star Rail FEST collection showcase. She then attempts to summon a Servant and use the Holy Grail’s power to leave this world. The overflowing Wishpower in this land restores the Holy Grail, and the King of Heroes Gilgamesh’s treasures are scattered across the arcadia. A massive commotion breaks out as a result. Upon the request of the Department of Aberration Defense, the Trailblazer works with Rin Tohsaka to locate the treasures and uncover the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. The special chapter and second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration officially begins on July 24. Please stay tuned.

As the Holy Grail War commences once more, Rin Tohsaka joins the team as a playable character. She is a 5-star Quantum-Type character following the Path of Erudition, skilled in using gems in battle. During combat, she gains a set amount of “Gem Energy” and deals multiple additional instances of DMG to a random target by consuming the “Gem Energy” she has. The more “Gem Energy” she consumes, the more instances of additional DMG she deals. When teammates consume or recover Skill Points, CRIT DMG also increases, and Rin Tohsaka accumulates “Gem Energy.”

Gilgamesh will also become a playable character. He is a 5-star Lightning-Type character following the Path of Destruction. After entering battle, each ally unit taking action can accumulate “Interest” for Gilgamesh, regenerate a set amount of Energy, and increase the DMG of his Ultimate. When accumulated “Interest” reaches its threshold, Gilgamesh unlocks his Skill and unleashes Gate of Babylon to fire weapons from his treasury, dealing multiple instances of DMG to enemies. When using his Ultimate, Gilgamesh summons Enuma Elish to deal massive DMG to all enemies.

From the start of the collaboration on July 24 until before Version 4.6 ends, Trailblazers can log into the game and claim Gilgamesh or Archer as one of the free limited 5-star collaboration characters, along with a portion of the corresponding upgrade materials for the character. The characters and Light Cones for this collaboration will be available long term, and the guarantee counts are shared between the Collaboration Character Warps of both phases. The same applies for the Collaboration Light Cone Warps. Spend a total of 200 Star Rail Special Passes in Version 4.4 to select and claim one of the limited 5-star collaboration Light Cone “I Am As You Behold” or “The Hell Where Ideals Burn” after the collaboration begins.

Lastly, Version 4.4 will also offer a variety of new events and game modes. First up, players can log in to claim a 10-pull reward once the version goes live. After the version update, the collaboration event “Fate/Star Rail Night” will also unfold as the story progresses. The Trailblazer tracks down the king’s treasures with Rin Tohsaka and gradually unravels the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. In the version event “Antigraft Brickbuster,” the Trailblazer takes on a request from Aventurine and launches an investigation into IPC corruption by stacking data bricks. Additionally, the permanent gameplay feature “Currency Wars: Zero-Sum Game” will be expanded and updated. Some system features will also be optimized.

Honkai: Star Rail is now available Free-To-Play on PC , iOS , Android , Epic Games Store , and PS5 . The In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound update will go live on July 15th 2026.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.4 Update Set for July 15
items.summary: HoYoverse revealed that Honkai: Star Rail's version 4.4 update will arrive in a couple of weeks. The update continues the story of Planarcadia, and also starts the second half of the game's collaboration with Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works].
items.content:
by Alex Fuller · Published July 3, 2026 · Updated July 3, 2026

HoYoverse announced that its version 4.4 update for Honkai: Star Rail will release on July 15, 2026. The update, titled “In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound”, continues the story of Planarcadia as players confront the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad and sees Himeko gaining a new playable form: the 5-star Fire-type Himeko • Nova. The update also begins the second half of the game’s collaboration with Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] from July 24, 2026. It will add both Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh as playable 5-star characters of Quantum-Type and Lightning-Type, respectively.

In addition, the version will include the return of limited 5-star characters Sparxie, Dan Heng • Permansor Terrae, and Evernight for a limited time during its first half, with Cerydra, Anaxa, and Aventurine returning to Warp banners in the second half. It will also include a new Antigraft Brickbuster event and an update for the Currency Wars: Zero-Sum Game mode.

Honkai: Star Rail is a free-to-play turn-based RPG that tells its own standalone story in the Honkai universe and is available on PC, PlayStation 5, iOS, and Android. The setting features Aeons, incarnations of universal principles that can freely travel between its worlds and apply their principles to them. Players embark on a journey on the Astral Express alongside other passengers looking to explore the universe and find out its secrets.

items.title: Honkai Star Rail 4.4 to give away a free 5-star hero from Fate/stay night
items.summary: Honkai Star Rail will feature characters from the popular franchise.
items.content:
0 Источник: miHoYo (HoYoverse) 03 Jul 20:52 Honkai Star Rail will feature characters from the popular franchise.

Recently, Honkai Star Rail developers held a special stream dedicated to update 4.4. This version was named "Whistle at the Edge of the World".

miHoYo (HoYoverse) talked about new content and also announced a new promotion: players in Honkai Star Rail will be able to get a free character of their choice in update 4.4 – both from the collaboration with the Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] franchise:

The character giveaway event in Honkai Star Rail 4.4 will start on July 24 and will last until the end of version 4.6:

During the event, Trailblazers can log in to the game and receive a joint exclusive character and materials for their development up to level 60 as a gift. 1 / 2 miHoYo (HoYoverse) During the 4.4 update presentation, miHoYo traditionally published several promo codes. Players will be able to get Stellar Jade and other bonuses in Honkai Star Rail by activating them – you need to act fast, as the codes expire quickly.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail - Official "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound" Version 4.4 Trailer
items.summary: Check out the new "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound" Version 4.4 trailer for the strategy RPG. Honkai: Star Rail is available now on PC, iOS, Android, and PlayStation 5 (PS5).

items.title: Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh Banners Open July 24; Honkai: Star Rail 4.4 Preview
items.summary: ©HoYoverseHoYoverse held a special program today (the 3rd) ahead of the 4.4 version update for its turn-based RPG, 'Honkai: Star Rail.'Version 4.4 will feature a decisive batt...
items.content:
HoYoverse held a special program today (the 3rd) ahead of the 4.4 version update for its turn-based RPG, 'Honkai: Star Rail.'

Version 4.4 will feature a decisive battle against the Lord Ravager, Asat Pramad, who has finally revealed their true colors. Before the showdown, the truth behind the Astral Express' emergency landing in the Ideal Paradise 15 years ago and Himeko's experiences in the Phantom Moon game will be unveiled. Asat Pramad will appear in three forms. In the first form, they unleash a barrage of rockets and strike energy spheres like billiard balls to deal massive AoE damage. Throughout the fight, players will engage in a dice game where they choose one buff while Asat Pramad takes the remaining two. Clearing the 4.4 Trailblaze Mission will add Asat Pramad to the Echo of War, allowing players to obtain the trace material 'Expectation of the False Sage.

Following this, Himeko's variant character, 'Himeko-Nova,' was revealed. She is a 5-star Fire-type character following the Path of Erudition. Upon triggering her trait, she enters battle by summoning the giant mech 'Star's Guide' and a domain boundary. When using her ultimate skill, she pilots the mech alongside her allies, attacking enemies with a high-frequency particle beam. By charging a gauge, she can unleash a powerful 'Orbital Annihilation Pulse.' Once the light blade attack count is exhausted, she delivers a finishing blow before returning to the battlefield.

When using her combat skill, she increases allies' damage and restores the usage count of 'Combat Support Skills.' Combat Support Skills are special abilities held by all allies within Himeko's domain; activating them summons the Star's Guide to attack all enemies. When the Express team summons the Star's Guide, additional effects are triggered based on the specific team members. The first half of version 4.4 will feature the Himeko-Nova banner alongside reruns for Sparkle, Dan Heng-Imbibitor Lunae, and Acheron. The second half will feature reruns for Kaelidra, Anaxa, and Aventurine.

Following the banner reveal, details for the second Fate/stay night UBW collaboration, 'Phantom Grail War,' were unveiled. Set in the Ideal Paradise rather than Penacony, the story begins as the Astral Express team donates various items—including the Holy Grail obtained in Penacony—to continue an exhibition after their battle with the Daybreak Squad. Upon hearing that the Grail has been stolen while preparing the exhibit, the team investigates based on testimony that a twin-tailed girl in red stole it. Meanwhile, Rin Tohsaka, who arrived in the Ideal Paradise after the 5th Holy Grail War, takes the Grail to return to her original world, only to be confused by the fact that it differs from the Grail she knows. The story kicks off as Gilgamesh also arrives in the Ideal Paradise to retrieve treasures scattered across the land.

Along with the Trailblaze Continuance story, the event content 'Fate/Night of the Galactic Railway' has also been revealed. In 'Fate/Night of the Galactic Railway,' Rin Tohsaka and the Trailblazer fight as a pair. In this mode, in addition to their basic skills, characters are granted various skills in the form of cards, similar to a roguelike deck-builder; when you defeat an enemy possessing a Noble Phantasm, you can acquire that Noble Phantasm as a card.

Next, the collaboration-exclusive 5-star characters, Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh, were introduced. Rin is a 5-star Quantum-type character on the Path of Erudition. She uses Bajiquan for her basic attack and the Jewel Sword Zelretch for her combat skill against single targets. She gains 'Jewel Energy' when using her technique, and can gain additional energy whenever allies use combat skills or healing abilities during battle.

When holding a certain amount of Jewel Energy, Rin's combat skill is enhanced, allowing her to deal damage to all enemies and then consume additional energy to strike random enemies multiple times. Her ultimate skill summons a giant bow to deal massive damage to all enemies. Furthermore, if Archer is in the party and specific conditions are met, a follow-up attack is triggered, and skill points are restored.

Gilgamesh is a 5-star Lightning-type character on the Path of Destruction. Upon entering battle, he only uses basic attacks until his 'Interest Gauge' is filled. The gauge charges whenever allies use ultimate skills. Once a certain threshold is reached, his combat skill 'Gate of Babylon' is unlocked, allowing him to consume the gauge to deal damage to all enemies. When teamed with Saber, he unleashes a barrage of weapons every time Saber attacks, performing a follow-up attack on all enemies. This increases his Interest Gauge, allowing for more frequent use of his combat skill. Using his technique 'Enkidu' restores his Interest Gauge, allowing him to use his combat skill sooner.

Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh will be available in banners starting July 24. With the start of the collaboration event, players will receive either Gilgamesh or Archer for free upon logging in. The second and first Fate/stay night event banners will share a pity count and will remain available for an extended period. Additionally, players who accumulate 200 pulls in any Warp event can select and receive a free signature Light Cone for either Gilgamesh or Archer.

Meanwhile, the 4.4 update will feature a mini-game event called 'Anti-Corruption Expert,' and four characters—Himeko-Nova, Acheron-Blade, Rin Tohsaka, and Gilgamesh—will be added to the Currency War. Two new Planar Ornaments and new emojis will be added, and Sparkle's new costume, 'Sweet Dream Propagation,' will be released in the shop. Several system optimizations are also planned, including batch level-up support for characters and Light Cones, a feature to claim all achievement rewards at once, and a search function for achievements.

Starting July 17, a collaboration with the immersive platform 'DEEP' will host an immersive exhibition. Visitors can enjoy various interactive content and purchase merchandise. The Honkai: Star Rail Immersive Exhibition will run from July 17 to August 31 at the immersive platform DEEP, located next to the Lotte World Ice Rink in Jamsil. Ticket reservations open on July 8 at 6:00 PM.

Honkai: Star Rail version 4.4 is scheduled for release on July 15. Detailed information can be found on the official lounge.

K/DA and Taric - Coa, Haeun, Yeovlynn, Rakang, Bong

items.title: Keep Your Trail Blazing With The UGREEN x Honkai: Star Rail Collaboration!
items.summary: miHoYo games are no stranger to the odd collaboration, be it with another title or even a car; however, UGREEN,...
items.content:
miHoYo games are no stranger to the odd collaboration, be it with another title or even a car; however, UGREEN, the consumer technology brand, seems to be ahead of the curve as their second collab is unveiled! Leaving the fantastical lands of Teyvat and entering the world of Honkai: Star Rail , the newest collab brings with it a cornucopia of on-the-go charging!

Titled "Power Up for the Next Trailblaze", the new range of products is themed around 5-star Elation character Yao Guan and consists of five main pieces of on-the-go gear: a 13.9mm ultra-thin magnetic power bank, a 65W fast charger, a 10,000mAh smart display fast-charging power bank, a 100W retractable fast-charging data cable, and an anti-loss tracker.

In addition to the aforementioned tech, the collaboration also provides some fun Yao Gaun-themed items, such as a chibi character stand, a transparent card, a badge, a holographic ticket, a lanyard, and a storage pouch!

The Honkai: Star Rail X UGREEN products are available now and will gradually roll out to online and offline retail channels worldwide.

GameGrin are proud to have all their articles researched, written, and edited by real people that care about gaming.

items.title: Ugreen x Honkai: Star Rail: Gaming Collaboration Unveiled
items.summary: Ugreen and Honkai: Star Rail are releasing five Yao Guang accessories plus a collector's box. Here's an overview of all the details on the power banks, charger, cable, and tracker.
items.content:
Share Link copied! (function() { window.afCopyLink = function(button, url) { if (navigator.clipboard && navigator.clipboard.writeText) { navigator.clipboard.writeText(url).then(function() { afShowSuccess(button); }).catch(function() { afFallbackCopy(url, button); }); } else { afFallbackCopy(url, button); } }; function afFallbackCopy(text, button) { var ta = document.createElement('textarea'); ta.value = text; ta.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;opacity:0'; document.body.appendChild(ta); ta.select(); try { document.execCommand('copy') && afShowSuccess(button); } catch (e) { alert('Bitte manuell kopieren: ' + text); } document.body.removeChild(ta); } function afShowSuccess(button) { var tooltip = button.querySelector('.copy-tooltip'); if (tooltip) { tooltip.classList.add('show'); } button.classList.add('copied'); setTimeout(function() { if (tooltip) { tooltip.classList.remove('show'); } button.classList.remove('copied'); }, 2000); } if (navigator.share) { var nativeBtn = document.querySelector('.af-share-native'); if (nativeBtn) { nativeBtn.style.display = 'flex'; nativeBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e) { e.preventDefault(); navigator.share({ title: document.title, url: window.location.href }); }); } } })(); Ugreen and HoYoverse are teaming up: The first officially licensed Ugreen x Honkai: Star Rail collection includes five charging accessories featuring Yao Guang designs, plus a limited-edition collector’s box. The series initially launched in the U.S., with prices ranging from $19.99 to $249.99. For fans, this means power banks, a GaN charger, a retractable cable, and a tracker—all styled after the Xianzhou General.

Following last year’s Genshin Impact collaboration, this marks Ugreen’s second foray into the HoYoverse universe. This time, the spotlight is on Yao Guang, a 5-star character introduced in Version 4.0. The series’ motto: “Power Up for the Next Trailblaze.”

For the designs, Ugreen has brought on board Yao Guang, the General of the Xianzhou Yuque. The designs draw inspiration from her role as an arbiter who interprets fate and turns misfortune into good fortune. But what matters isn’t just the aesthetics—it’s also the technology behind them.

This magnetic power bank offers 10,000 mAh and can charge up to three devices simultaneously, both wired and wirelessly. An integrated USB-C cable doubles as a carrying strap. Price in the U.S.: $89.99.

Image: Ugreen Nexode Pro Power Bank Also with 10,000 mAh, but featuring up to 45 W fast charging, an integrated USB-C cable, and a smart display that shows battery level and charging status. Price: $59.99. This is the only model not included in the collector’s box.

Image: Ugreen Nexode Air Charger The GaN charger delivers up to 65 W via a single USB-C port and, according to Ugreen, is about 46 percent more compact than standard 65-W power adapters. The foldable prongs make it ideal for travel. Price: $49.99.

Image: Ugreen Nexode Retractable USB-C Cable The retractable cable delivers up to 100 W, making it suitable for laptops as well. Data transfers at speeds of up to 480 Mbit/s. At $19.99, it’s the most affordable individual product in the series.

Image: Ugreen FineTrack Mini 2 The tracker works with Apple’s “Find My” network to locate lost items. A 600-mAh CR2450 battery is rated to last for up to 2,700 search operations, and a 110-dB signal helps you locate the item. Price: $21.99.

The limited-edition Collectible Gift Box includes four of the five devices: the MagFlow Air Power Bank, the Nexode Air Charger, the retractable USB-C cable, and the FineTrack Mini 2. It also includes exclusive Yao Guang merchandise: an acrylic display stand, a metal pin, a holographic ticket, and a pouch for the power bank. The box opens like a peacock’s tail, which fits the theme perfectly.

Anyone familiar with the Ugreen x Genshin Impact Edition already knows roughly what to expect: solid charging technology in a package aimed more at fans than at bargain hunters.

The catch for local Trailblazers: Ugreen has so far only announced the series for the U.S. There, it’s available for purchase on Amazon, the official Ugreen store, and the TikTok Shop. Additionally, Ugreen will be showcasing the products alongside Honkai: Star Rail at Anime Expo in Los Angeles from July 2–5, 2026, where they’ll be sold at the official merch booth.

Image: Ugreen A date for a German market launch has not yet been announced. However, with the Genshin collaboration, it didn’t take long for the accessories to appear here as well on de.ugreen.com and Amazon.de. A similar pattern is therefore conceivable, though it’s not guaranteed.

If you’re already looking for a portable battery and aren’t set on the fan-inspired design, it’s worth checking out our roundup of the best power banks . And to see how Ugreen’s standard magnetic power bank performs in everyday use, check out our review of the MagFlow Power Bank .

A German launch date has not yet been confirmed. Currently, the collection is only available in the U.S.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.3 guide: Events, free Jades, & banner schedule
Giving a game another chance
items.summary: Honkai: Star Rail (HSR) celebrated its three-year anniversary on April 26, 2026. I originally started playing back when it first came out, but I stopped not too long after because I didn’t really like it. That was because I had just been playing a different game from HoYoverse, the company that makes HSR, that I...
items.content:
Honkai: Star Rail (HSR) celebrated its three-year anniversary on April 26, 2026. I originally started playing back when it first came out, but I stopped not too long after because I didn’t really like it.

That was because I had just been playing a different game from HoYoverse, the company that makes HSR, that I had to stop playing because of bugs. I also stopped because it was really laggy, but I realized when I started playing again this past December that I had actually originally played on the European server rather than the American one, which was why it was lagging.

Now that I’ve started playing it again for the last four months, I’m wishing I’d never quit.

The game’s story is amazing. With games like HSR where the main character is a self-insert, I tend to fully lean into that role. As such, whenever a major twist happens to a character that the main character has bonded with, I really feel it due to the immersion.

Another thing I like about the story is that HSR keeps you on your toes because it isn’t afraid to kill off playable characters.

I’d played a different game from HoYoverse before HSR, and it wouldn’t dream of killing a playable character, so I went into HSR thinking it’d be the same. That idea went out the window on my second day of playing with the first of many playable character deaths.

Because of that, you end up wanting to make sure you own your favorite characters in case you never see them in the game again. However, since HSR is a gacha game, you’re not guaranteed to get the characters you want when they’re available without spending money.

At least that’s what you’d think, but from my experience, HSR is the easiest gacha game I’ve played when it comes to getting the characters you want without spending money.

I’ve managed to get three characters I’ve wanted along with two of their exclusive items without spending money. That’s because HSR makes it really easy to get the in-game currency for pulling characters. That means if you just want to get a character for the sake of having them, you can.

In fact, I’d say HSR is probably the only gacha game where you don’t have to spend real money. I only spent money on cosmetics, but most cosmetics are free rewards from permanent events.

The only negative thing I have to say about the game is that the newer main quests are fairly long.

The latest finished questline takes 62 hours to complete, and the entire main quest line is currently over 100 hours. Because of that, if you have a busy daily schedule, it could take a while to complete. It is possible to finish in a week, but based on personal experience, I wouldn’t recommend it.

The length aside, I would highly recommend this game, especially since it’s free on PC and you don’t even need a gaming one as I play on my laptop. With the three years of content in the game so far, I can’t wait to see where it goes from here.

items.title: Hands on with the Honkai: Star Rail v4.0 experience
items.summary: This HoyoVerse train just keeps on chugging!
items.content:
The last article I wrote for Honkai: Star Rail was going over the details of the upcoming Version 4.0 release . Since then, HoYoverse generously offered up the opportunity to play Version 4.0 and experience Planarcadia early. Having spent about a dozen hours or so playing with the new characters, experiencing the story, and generally taking in the sights and sounds of Planarcadia, I’m prepared to share my thoughts on all of it.

It’s best to start off with the main attraction of Version 4.0: Planarcadia. This new planet is a fictional world come to life and boy does it leave a fantastic first impression. Full of the kind of sights and sounds you’d expect from an Asian city brimming with life. Exploration is a blast with all the alleys and little nooks to find, the platforming segments that can either help you navigate across certain parts of the city or find chests within them. There’s also a new style of puzzles that require you to rotate a 2D plane in a 3D space to “paint” certain parts of it to make them solid and let you platform with those objects. Planarcadia is so full of whimsy and still has some of the overhang of a crime family really enabling a super unique feeling planet compared to what we’ve had with previous locations such as the Xianzhou or Amphoreus.

The two new characters following the path of Elation feel great, even if the Aha Moment is a little overstimulating sometimes. Sparxie and Yao Guang fit on a team together like a glove to demolish all the bosses I’ve thrown them against. I’m eager to see where the new path goes with time, especially since we only have the two characters currently. Even as of the live public server branch, we’re unsure when Elation Trailblazer will make their debut, so keep an eye on that as versions progress beyond 4.0. You’ll need to get a hold of new Elation Light Cones as well since they obviously can’t use the other Paths’ options. Burning skill points to increase the Elation stacks until Aha decides he’s satisfied and steps in for a big attack feels really great and it has me curious what future Elation characters might do when they trigger their own Aha Moments.

Not to mention that for new players, you’ve got the opportunity to really kickstart a team of your choice with a new planet. We’re at a point in the game where there’s so many options for characters you can pick from and build around, and 4.0 introducing Elation means that as a new player, you can jump in and get a headstart on pulling for that archetype. Especially with a new Novaflare round of buffs, there’s even more options than before bringing older characters back into the limelight. The Black Swan and Sparkle buffs are so massive that people running DoT teams or any team using an action advance support such as Bronya or Sunday have another insane option. Sparkle was always good, but her Novaflare enables her to further buff your DPS units to extreme levels. Black Swan’s Novaflare was even more insane and rocketed her back up to the top for damage even as a DoT unit. Combined with the option to pick a free character including units like Sparkle, Kafka (who works great in DoT teams), and more, you really can’t go wrong in Planarcadia and the Version 4.0 patch from what I’ve gotten to experience.

Between the free characters, the great change of pace in the story from the latter half of Amphoreus (if you’ve played it, you know), the new cityscape that is quite honestly my new favorite location in the game, and all the new characters to look forward to, you really should make a return to Star Rail if you’ve been away for a minute and are curious about where we’re at. If you’re new, definitely consider giving it a shot. There’s even a bonus to let you convert trace materials for leveling character skills at a 1 to 1 ratio this time instead of a 2 for 1, so you really can kickstart your Elation team. With characters being important again like Silver Wolf, Blade, and Himeko, as well as new faces like Sparxie, Yao Guang, Ashveil, and Pearl, I think 4.0 is just the start of a real treat of a patch cycle for Honkai: Star Rail.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.0 impressions
items.content:
I'll admit, I recently burned out on Honkai: Star Rail. Upon hearing the news that 3.8 would be the lengthiest patch yet and with content more sparse than ever before, I decided to take a break for the first time since I started playing all the way back in 1.0. The entire Amphoreus saga left me feeling drained; I thoroughly enjoyed 3.0-3.4 as excellent set-up chapters, but felt lost as the plot became a labyrinth of hidden motivations, layered twists, hours of exposition, and endless proper nouns. When looking at the story as a whole, I love the characters (Aglaea and Anaxa in particular) and the rich history of the world within the Scepter, but the later patches often felt like studying for a needlessly convoluted test. I understood the gist of what was happening, but I won't lie to you and say I could describe to you in detail what Cyrene's deal is or how Evernight fits into everything.

All that's to say: I was looking forward to the Astral Express crew taking a well-earned vacation to Planarcadia, the planet of Elation. Despite two of the game's four current versions ostensibly beginning with Akivili's disciples deciding that they need a break from the backbreaking work of saving the universe, neither has resulted in our trailblazers seeing much downtime. Unlike previous excursions, it's all hands on deck: everyone gets off at this stop (save for Black Swan's incredibly hasty and unfortunate exit prior to departure). It's fun to see the entire main cast get involved, none more welcome than Himeko — up until now, the only character trait I could've told you about her was that she's terrible at making coffee. Getting some elaboration on her backstory and giving her a personality outside of "Express mom" is long overdue.

The Trailblazer and March 7th begin their Planarcadian getaway with a suitably devious greeting by none other than our old pal Sparkle. We're given a tour of the vibrant, totally-not-Japanese-inspired streets, crawling with humans living in tentative harmony with totally-not-Penaconian-memetic-entities known as imagenae. Here, we catch a glimpse of what I hope will become one of Planarcadia's core themes: gentrification and the plight of capitalism, with a side of anti-imperialism (all perpetrated by the IPC, of course). It's bold, though, and I worry that future patches will keep these criticisms in the background rather than highlight them as Planarcadia's core issues. I would love so much for little nuggets of lore to be elaborated upon, ones that suggest a more serious political history than Aha would have you believe: what did the imagenae do before the IPC forced them to integrate into society? Were they not allowed to work at all? Were they marginalized and seen as second-class citizens? Is the Department of Aberration Defense an inherently evil organization left to determine on their own which imagenae deserve their continued existence and which do not? Is Kuchiba so hot that it doesn't even matter?

This brings us to the Synwish Syndicate, a gang formerly designed to be a respite for imagenae that feel rejected elsewhere, now, due to IPC intervention, a gang that largely exists to enable monetary gain by questionable means. Here at the syndicate's headquarters, the plot quickly begins to balloon into what feels like a collection of introductions for what's to come. We have Pearl's cold and calculating grip on the planet, Himeko and her confounding family history, the potential return of the Herald of Death, the Stellaron Hunters' unknown involvement in the Phantasmoon Games, Sunday's inadvertent entry into the Games, Evanescia's whole...thing, and Yao Guang's bottomless supply of divinations. What's most shocking is that the patch still manages to pull off a fun (if bloated) self-contained story, adding layers to one of HSR's most inscrutable characters and ending things with one of the game's best low-stakes twists.

I'm no Xianzhou Arbiter-General, but I can pretty easily peer into the future and divine a prophecy for Honkai: Star Rail: each new patch will introduce and/or discuss at length a new prophecy that cryptically divulges the fate of a character and/or planet and/or simulation masquerading as a planet. It's hard to pinpoint the moment a theme becomes a crutch, but I think HSR passed that moment long ago. In this patch, we have not one, not two, but three central characters that are defined by their relationship with prophecy and prediction — Yao Guang, Pearl, and the perennial mystery that is Elio and his script. So many in-game conversations devolve into the interpretation of prophecy, and frankly, it's becoming exhausting. We went through an entire version in which each Chrysos Heir had a poetically vague predetermined fate, and before that (and in 3.8's ret-con-y confusion) we had Sparkle's ambiguous "three deaths" for Firefly.

If this were a wholesale replacement for well-executed foreshadowing, we would have a far bigger issue. It's obvious, though, that the writing team knows how to tell a story sans-prophecy, evident in Sparkle's character-driven narrative in this very patch. Sparxie being the "true" Sparkle (as much as old Sparkle is also the "true" Sparkle) is teased so perfectly; the reveal is obvious in retrospect while being difficult to predict with the given information. It's consistently groan-inducing to see this writing cheat code being used ad nauseam within a story that could function better without it. I hope I'll be proven wrong and the game will subvert the words of its murky prophets, leaving me with egg on my face come the conclusion of this leg of the Express' journey.

4.0 is a promising new beginning, and has at the very least brought me back in terms of its story. Though there is an abundance of new proper nouns to memorize and a bevy of new and returning cast members to keep track of, the patch still takes on a welcome return to simplicity and levity (save for the shocking tonal shift right at the end, RIP the Synwish Syndicate Feb. 2026 - Feb. 2026). As long as things don't get too bogged down in extensive metaphors and tiresome exposition, I'm hopeful that Planarcadia can become the most focused version of HSR we've seen so far.
